content: For a controllable canvas game: define state first, expose it on window, wire input to a single keys/pressed object, update movement with capped dt, draw layers in order, then add restart and game-over. Keep input/update/draw reading the same state.

recipe.state: state={player:{x,y,vx,vy},entities[],score,over,paused} on window
traps: keydown auto-repeat double-fires actions: track keys[code], act on press edge only
update and draw reading different state copies: one window.state, everything reads it
RAF not cancelled on restart: store the id, cancelAnimationFrame before reset or loops double
uncapped dt after tab blur teleports everything: dt = Math.min(dt, 0.05)
game-over/stun checks placed above timer decrements leave states stuck: tick timers first
probes: arrow key changes state.player.x
state.score increments on pickup
R flips state.over back to false

content: For a turn-based board game: store board[r][c], render row/col only at draw boundaries, implement select then legal-target commit, block input while animating/AI thinking, and expose a small game API for probes.

recipe.state: board[r][c] (0 empty | piece id); turn; selected{r,c}|null; legalMoves[]; over; winner
order: no selection: select own piece, compute legalMoves
traps: row/col vs x/y mixups: store board[row][col], convert to pixels only at draw boundaries
input accepted while animating or AI thinking: block with a busy flag
highlights drawn after pieces hide them: board, then highlights, then pieces
AI computed synchronously freezes the UI: wrap in setTimeout(.., 50-300)
forgetting turn switch on capture branches: one commitMove() path for every legal move
probes: clicking own piece sets state.selected
legal move mutates board AND flips state.turn
illegal target leaves board unchanged

content: For an asset-backed animated game: request base sprites early, load/decode them before first draw, use drawImage for generated entities, track animation timers in state, and provide procedural fallbacks only when asset generation is unavailable.

recipe.state: ASSETS{name:Image}; per entity {action, frame, frameTimer}
order: load + await img.decode() for all sprites
traps: drawImage before decode finishes: blank or flickering sprites - gate the loop on load
code-drawn shapes layered over loaded sprites: pick ONE representation per entity
regenerating art to fix a pose never works: flip/offset existing frames instead
frame index overflow when action changes: reset frame=0 on action switch, always mod length
art faces left while flip math assumes right: author right-facing, ctx.scale(-1,1) to face left
probes: every ASSETS entry has naturalWidth>0
entity frame index changes within 500 ms
canvas non-blank after first draw

content: For a tile-based grid game where the player navigates corridors and cannot pass through walls: define state.maze[row][col] at build time (0=corridor, 1=wall, 2=door, 3=exit). Position the player by tile coords (state.player.tx, ty); derive pixel coords (px=tx*TILE_SIZE) at draw time. Movement: on keydown, check maze[nextTy][nextTx] !== 1 BEFORE applying. Draw: nested loop over the maze for walls/corridors, then sprites on top. Probes: maze rendered (canvas non-blank), player not in wall (the canvas-grid-navigation recipe's auto_probe checks this), keydown moves player by exactly 1 tile.

recipe.state: maze[r][c] (0 corridor,1 wall,...); player{tx,ty,dir}; enemies[]{tx,ty,dir}; score; over
order: at tile centers accept turn if open
traps: moving then checking walls: validate the target tile first or the player enters walls
pixel coords stored in state: keep tile coords (tx,ty), derive px=tx*TILE only at draw
enemies flip 180 forever: forbid reversing unless at a dead-end
digging drawn as an overlay: carve by mutating the maze cell value itself
edge off-by-one: clamp 0..cols-1/0..rows-1 or wrap explicitly, never both
probes: keydown moves player exactly 1 tile
player tile is never a wall
score increments on pellet/dig event

content: For a side-scrolling platformer: state.platforms[] with {x, y, w, h} entries; state.player = {x, y, vx, vy, w, h, onGround}. Gravity each frame: player.vy += GRAVITY (e.g., 0.5); player.y += player.vy. Collision AFTER movement: iterate platforms; if player overlaps a platform AND was moving down (vy > 0), snap player.y = platform.y - player.h; player.vy = 0; player.onGround = true. Jump on Space: IF player.onGround THEN player.vy = -JUMP_POWER (e.g., -10). Camera-follow: subtract (player.x - canvas.width/2) from every draw x. Probes: gravity works (vy > 0 after a frame in air), can jump (player.y decreases after Space), can land (onGround flips false→true).

recipe.state: player{x,y,vx,vy,w,h,onGround}; platforms[]{x,y,w,h}; camera.x; enemies[]; goal
traps: jump allowed mid-air: require onGround, set it false immediately on jump
landing without zeroing vy: snap y = platform.y - player.h AND vy = 0 or it jitters
camera offset applied to some draws only: subtract camera.x in every world-space draw
collision checked before movement: integrate first, then resolve, or you tunnel
onGround stays true after walking off a ledge: clear each frame, set only on landing
probes: vy>0 after one airborne frame
Space decreases player.y
onGround flips false->true on landing

content: For a 3D first-person game: STRONGLY PREFER three.js via CDN over hand-rolling a raycaster — THREE.WebGLRenderer + Scene + Camera + Mesh ships real 3D in <500 lines. State: state.player = {x, y, z, yaw, pitch}; camera.rotation.order = 'YXZ'. Input: pointer-lock on canvas click; mouse moves yaw/pitch; ArrowUp moves forward along yaw vector (sin(yaw), 0, -cos(yaw)). PICK ONE convention for forward and use it identically for movement, billboards, lookAt — sign-flips between movement and camera produce 'walking opposite from facing' bugs (see playbook fps-camera-and-movement-vectors). Weapon view as screen-space overlay (parented to camera OR HTML ), NOT a world-space mesh. Probes: yaw exposed on window.state; pointer-lock requested on click; up-arrow changes player.x or .z (not both).

recipe.state: player{x,y,z,yaw,pitch,hp}; enemies[]; THREE scene/camera/renderer
order: keys move along forward=(sin yaw,0,-cos yaw)
traps: forward sign differs between movement and camera: ONE convention everywhere or you walk backwards
weapon as world-space mesh: parent it to the camera or use an HTML overlay img
billboard sprites not re-aimed each frame: call lookAt(camera.position) every frame
pitch unclamped flips the view: clamp to +-Math.PI/2*0.95
pointer-lock requested outside a user gesture silently fails: request inside the click handler
probes: state.yaw exposed on window
canvas click requests pointer lock
ArrowUp changes player.x or .z (not both equally)

content: For a top-down action game (camera looks straight down): state.player = {x, y, facing} where facing is an angle in radians (or one of N/E/S/W). Free 8-direction movement: on keydown set vx/vy per pressed key; normalize diagonals (vx /= sqrt(2), same for vy). Enemies as objects {x, y, behavior}; simple AI = step toward player each tick. Projectiles spawn at player position with velocity matching facing. Probes: arrow-key movement changes player.x or .y; collision detected between projectile and enemy (AABB or circle-overlap).

recipe.state: player{x,y,facing}; bullets[]; enemies[]; spawnTimer; score; lives; over
traps: bullets spawn every held frame: fire on cooldown or keydown edge, not key state
diagonal movement 41% faster: divide vx,vy by sqrt(2) when both axes pressed
splicing arrays while iterating forward skips entries: iterate backwards or filter
ship thrust sign: vx=Math.cos(angle)*speed, vy=Math.sin(angle)*speed - verify on screen
asteroids drawn as perfect circles: build irregular polygons from jittered radii
tuning: fire cooldown 150-300 ms, spawns every 1-3 s
probes: arrows change player.x/.y
fire key grows bullets[]
enemy count drops after a hit

content: For a grid puzzle game: state.board[row][col] as 2D array of cell values (0=empty or a piece type). Tetris-shape: piece position + rotation + shape matrix; on tick, attempt to move down; on collision, freeze + scan full rows for clear. 2048-shape: shift rows in input direction, merge adjacent equal values once per move, spawn a new tile at a random empty cell. Match-3 shape: after a swap, scan for 3+ in a row/col; clear matches; pieces above drop into gaps; cascade. Probes: board is 2D array on state; legal input mutates the board; score increases on clear/merge.

recipe.state: board[r][c]; piece{shape,x,y,rot}; next; score; over; dropTimer
order: on collision lock piece into board
traps: rotating without re-checking collision overlaps walls: test the rotated shape first
clearing rows top-down while splicing skips rows: scan and remove bottom-up
2048 merging a tile twice in one move: mark merged cells until move resolves
spawn-position collision must end the game, not crash or overlap
input still mutating the board after game over: gate all input on !state.over
tuning: tetris gravity 0.8 s/row, faster per level
probes: state.board is a 2D array
legal input mutates board
score increases on clear/merge

content: For a pseudo-3D road racer: state.road is an array of segments (z-distances) each with curve + hill values. Render bottom-up: project each segment to screen y via perspective formula, draw road quad. Player sprite stays centered at the bottom; horizon shifts left/right with curves. Steering: ArrowLeft/Right adjusts player.x relative to road center, clamped to road width. Speed: throttle adds to z; friction subtracts. Probes: state.player.speed > 0 after ArrowUp held; road segments visible (canvas non-blank); steering changes player.x.

recipe.state: road segments[]{curve,hill}; pos (z along track); player{x offset, speed}; cars[]
order: steering shifts player.x scaled by speed
traps: drawing road front-to-back: paint far segments first or nothing layers correctly
steering active at zero speed: scale steering by speed/maxSpeed
curve offsets accumulated across frames: recompute x/dx from the segment list every render
mixing screen px with world z units in the projection: project once, one formula
player.x unclamped: off-road needs slowdown or clamp, not flight
tuning: max speed reached in 3-6 s of throttle
probes: state.player.speed>0 while ArrowUp held
canvas non-blank with road drawn
steering changes player.x

content: For a paddle-ball game: state.ball = {x, y, vx, vy, r}; state.paddle = {x, y, w}; Breakout variant adds state.bricks[]. Each frame: ball.x += ball.vx; ball.y += ball.vy; bounce off walls (if x<0 || x>canvas.width then vx*=-1); bounce off paddle (if ball.y>paddle.y && ball.x within paddle.x range then vy*=-1); brick collision iterates bricks; on hit splice the brick + ball.vy*=-1. Paddle input: ArrowLeft/Right OR mouseX. Probes: ball moves (state.ball.x changes after a frame); ball bounces off walls; paddle responds to input.

recipe.state: ball{x,y,vx,vy,r}; paddle{x,w}; bricks[]{x,y,w,h,alive}; lives; score; over
order: paddle bounce, angle from hit offset
traps: ball trapped inside paddle: bounce only when vy>0 AND reposition ball above the paddle
two bricks hit in one frame double-reverse vy: break after the first brick hit
pure vertical loops: derive vx from (ball.x - paddle.center)/half-width on paddle hits
speed creep after many bounces: clamp total ball speed to a max
serve auto-launches: glue ball to paddle until launch input
tuning: ball 250-400 px/s, +5% per paddle hit, cap ~600
probes: ball.x changes each frame
wall contact flips vx sign
paddle responds to input

content: For a lane-crossing game: state.lanes[] each with {direction, speed, obstacles[]}. Player advances toward target row by one lane per ArrowUp keypress. Each frame: advance all obstacles by lane.speed * lane.direction; wrap off-edge to opposite side. Collision: if any obstacle's bounds overlap player position → lose a life. Score: number of safe crossings. Probes: lanes rendered (canvas non-blank); player moves on Up (state.player.row decreases); obstacles cycle (state.lanes[i].obstacles[0].x changes over time).

recipe.state: lanes[]{y,dir,speed,obstacles[]}; player{col,row}; lives; score; over
order: keydown edge moves player one row/col
wrap off-edge to the far side
river lanes: logs carry, water kills
traps: holding Up crosses in one frame: move one row per keydown edge, not key state
player stored in rows but collision in pixels: convert once, compare in one unit
river logic inverted: ON a log is safe, in water without a log is death
wrapped obstacles pop in at the edge: respawn just off-screen
all lanes same speed/direction feels dead: vary both per lane
tuning: obstacles 60-180 px/s, faster on higher levels
probes: Up decreases state.player.row
lanes[i].obstacles[0].x changes over time
reaching the top increments score

content: For a point-and-click adventure: state.scenes (each with background + hotspots[] of {x, y, w, h, on_click}). Mouse click hit-tests against the active scene's hotspots; matched hotspot fires its on_click (changes scene, gives item, plays dialog). Use canvas.getBoundingClientRect() to map clientX/Y → canvas coords. Inventory: state.inventory[] of item ids; render as a HUD strip. Probes: clicking a hotspot fires a state change (state.scene changes OR state.inventory grows); cursor shape changes over hotspots. Include a visible help/instructions overlay or ? button for controls and puzzle verbs; expose enough state for probes to verify an inventory-item-on-hotspot puzzle chain.

recipe.state: scene id; scenes{id:{bg,hotspots[]{x,y,w,h,on_click}}}; inventory[]; dialog{open,lines,idx}; helpOverlay{open}; selectedItem|null
order: hit-test active scene hotspots, topmost first
select inventory item -> click compatible hotspot -> apply puzzle result
H or ? toggles help overlay; Escape closes it
hover sets cursor style; draw bg, inventory HUD, dialog/help box
traps: raw clientX/Y on a CSS-scaled canvas: always map through the bounding rect
hit-testing a stale hotspot list after scene change: read the active scene list each click
clicks pass through dialog/help to hotspots: consume input while overlays are open
items collectible twice: mark taken in state and re-check on entry
invisible hotspots are unfindable: change cursor (or subtle glow) on hover
dig/use puzzle checks bare hotspot clicks: require selectedItem plus correct hotspot
tuning: dialog text 18-24 px on a dark panel
probes: hotspot click changes state.scene or grows state.inventory
cursor changes over a hotspot
H/? opens helpOverlay; selected item + matching hotspot changes scene/dialog/inventory

content: For an isometric tile grid: store world as tiles[row][col] of type ids. Convert (col, row) → screen with iso projection: sx = (col - row) * TILE_W/2; sy = (col + row) * TILE_H/2. Render row-by-row from top so depth ordering is correct (later rows draw on top of earlier). Click handling: invert the iso projection — col = (sx/(TILE_W/2) + sy/(TILE_H/2))/2; row = (sy/(TILE_H/2) - sx/(TILE_W/2))/2. Probes: tiles is 2D array on state; clicking a tile sets state.selected to its (col, row).

recipe.state: tiles[r][c]; selected{col,row}|null; entities[]{col,row}; origin offset
order: draw back-to-front from origin (canvas.width/2, top margin)
traps: origin offset missing: sx=(col-row)*TILE_W/2 needs +canvas.width/2 or all is off-screen left
drawing in array order not (row+col) order: depth glitches - sort or iterate diagonally
click inversion forgetting the same origin offset used when projecting
entity pixel positions cached: derive from (col,row) every frame so hops stay aligned
tall tile art clipped: anchor sprites at the tile's bottom edge, not its top
probes: state.tiles is a 2D array
clicking a tile sets state.selected to its (col,row)

content: For an overworld RPG: tile map for terrain; NPCs as objects {x, y, dialogue, behavior}; player movement on a grid (snap to tile each step, no in-between pixels). Talking: when player faces an NPC and presses Action, advance dialogue state (state.dialogue = {npc, line_index}). Battle / scene transitions: state.scene as string ('overworld' | 'battle' | 'menu'); switch the active draw routine on change. Probes: map rendered; arrow keys move player tile by tile (state.player.tx changes by 1); pressing Action near an NPC opens dialogue (state.dialogue.open === true).

recipe.state: map[r][c]; player{tx,ty,facing,moving}; npcs[]{tx,ty,dialogue}; scene; dialogue{open,idx}
order: keydown edge starts a one-tile move
animate to target tile, snap on arrival
Action key checks the FACED tile for an NPC
traps: free pixel movement: commit one tile per input and snap - no in-between resting states
Action checks the player's own tile: test the tile the player FACES
movement still processed while dialogue open: gate on dialogue.open first
NPCs walkable-through: treat their tiles as solid in the move check
scene changes but the old draw keeps running: switch draw routine inside ONE loop
probes: arrows change player.tx/ty by exactly 1
Action facing an NPC sets dialogue.open===true
map renders non-blank

content: For a vertical platformer with ladders: state.platforms[] with y-positions; state.ladders[] with {x, yTop, yBot} where yTop and yBot DERIVE from platform.y values (NOT hardcoded pixels — drift otherwise). Hazards (barrels, falling enemies) need a respawn LOOP: each frame, advance and remove off-screen entries; spawn a new one every N seconds. Climbing: when player.x is inside a ladder column AND a keyDown is held, ignore gravity and adjust y by climb speed. Probes: at least 2 platforms exist; ladders touch a platform above and a platform below (the canvas-vertical-platformer recipe's auto_probe checks this); hazards keep coming (state.totalSpawned > state.hazards.length over time).

recipe.state: platforms[]{x,y,w}; ladders[]{x,yTop,yBot}; player{x,y,vy,onGround,onLadder}; hazards[]; spawnTimer; lives; totalSpawned
order: ladder column check: gravity off, vertical input moves
spawn hazard every N s (loop, not one-shot)
traps: ladder yTop/yBot hardcoded in pixels: DERIVE from platform.y values or they drift apart
hazard spawner runs once: it must be a repeating loop that keeps barrels coming
hazard collision only checked on the spawn row: check on every row each frame
climbing past the ladder top into air: clamp at yTop and snap onto the platform
jump while onLadder double-states: leaving a ladder clears onLadder before jump physics
probes: at least 2 platforms exist
every ladder touches a platform above and below
state.totalSpawned grows over time

content: For a 2-character fighting / dueling game: state.players = [p1, p2] each with {x, y, facing, state, hp}. INITIAL positions: p1 on the left half, p2 on the right half. Facing convention: left-player.facing = +1 (points right), right-player.facing = -1 (points left). Sprite draw: respect facing via a single ctx.scale(facing, 1) wrapper inside save/restore. If the user later asks 'flip just the punch direction' or similar, the MINIMUM change is to wrap ONLY that state's drawcall — do NOT restructure the facing state machine (see playbook scope-flip-minimal-change). /vlm-critique uses checklist `canvas-two-actors-facing` (Q3 facing, Q5 attack pose). Probes: both players visible; both players face each other (canvas-two-actors-facing has an auto_actors_face_each_other_strict probe); attack input causes a state change AND a visible attack pixel in front of the attacker.

recipe.state: players[2]{x,y,vx,vy,facing,action,actionTimer,hp}; over; winner; roundTimer
order: actionTimer -= dt FIRST; at <=0 return action to idle
attack hitbox vs opponent only on active frames, once per attack
facing = sign(opponent.x - x) in ONE place; draw via ctx.scale(facing,1)
traps: timer decrement below `if action==='hit' return`: the hit player NEVER recovers - tick timers before any early-return
facing derived in 2+ places: drift makes kicks land backwards - one derivation, draw-time flip only
attack damages every overlap frame: set a hasHit flag per attack, clear on idle
both players reading one keys object without per-player key maps
sprite art faces left but flip math assumes right-facing: author right, scale(-1,1) for left
tuning: hit stun 200-400 ms, attack active 80-150 ms
probes: both players visible and facing each other
attack changes action AND lands hp damage in range
after stun the hit player moves again (control recovery)

content: For particle / VFX / fluid sandbox: state.particles[] of {x, y, vx, vy, life, color}. Each frame: update every particle (advance position, decay life), remove dead ones, spawn from emitters. Render with low alpha (ctx.globalAlpha=0.6) for blending; use additive composite (ctx.globalCompositeOperation='lighter') for fire/lasers. Cap particles.length to prevent FPS death (e.g., MAX_PARTICLES = 800; drop oldest when over). Probes: at least N particles exist after T seconds; particle count is stable (not growing unboundedly).

recipe.state: particles[]{x,y,vx,vy,life,maxLife,color,size}; emitters[]; spawnAccumulator
traps: unbounded particle growth kills FPS: hard cap (drop oldest) every frame
splice inside a forward loop skips neighbors: iterate backwards
globalAlpha/composite not reset after draw: save/restore or reset explicitly each frame
per-particle gradient objects allocate per frame: precompute styles per color
spawning Math.floor(rate*dt) drops fractions at high FPS: accumulate the remainder
probes: particles.length >= N after T seconds
particle count stays bounded over time

content: For a city / economy builder: tile-based world + resource pools (state.resources = {money, power, water, ...}) + buildings[] with {type, x, y, level, output}. Each tick: every building generates output and consumes inputs; insufficient inputs degrade output. Build menu: cursor + selected building type; click places if (affordable AND tile is free). Probes: resource counter updates per tick; placing a building costs resources; rendered tiles match the tiles[][] data.

recipe.state: tiles[r][c]; resources{money,power,...}; buildings[]{type,col,row,level}; selectedType; tickAccum
order: economy tick every T s: produce/consume per building
draw tiles, buildings, ghost cursor, HUD
traps: economy run per frame: use a fixed tick (e.g. 1/s) or numbers explode with FPS
placement ignoring occupancy: track building presence in tiles[][]
resources go negative: validate cost BEFORE deducting
ghost/cursor preview drawn into the world state: separate draw pass, never stored
float drift on resources: keep integers internally, format at display
probes: a resource counter changes every tick
placing a building deducts resources
rendered buildings match tiles[][] data

content: For a space-trading / exploration game: state.systems[] of {id, x, y, market: {commodity → price}}. Player ship has {x, y, fuel, cargo, money}. Travel = navigate to a system (consumes fuel). Market: buy low / sell high; prices vary per system. Combat (optional): enemy ships with HP + simple chase-or-flee AI. Probes: at least N systems generated on init; navigating consumes fuel (state.player.fuel decreases); buying changes cargo AND money.

recipe.state: systems[]{id,x,y,market{good:price}}; ship{fuel,cargo{},money}; currentSystem; mode('map'|'market')
order: map mode: click a system within fuel range
buy/sell validates money, hold space, stock
draw starmap OR market exclusively by mode
traps: stranding: never allow a jump that leaves no reachable refuel - check before commit
no cargo cap: enforce hold size or one trade breaks the economy
identical prices everywhere: spread +-40% per system or trading is pointless
float money accumulates errors: integer credits only
both map and market drawn in the same frame: exclusive draw per mode flag
tuning: fuel for 3-5 jumps, refuel costs money
probes: systems.length >= N on init
travel decreases ship.fuel
buy moves money into cargo

content: QTE/cutscene game. Script SCENES=[{id,bg,frames,qte:{expectKey,prompt,cueMs,windowStartMs,durationMs,hitMs},hazardPath:{from,to,threatens}}]. Draw generated bg_* PNGs first with drawImage; rect backgrounds are fallback only. Telegraph timing: windup during cueMs, then move the threat along hazardPath to the threatened part by hitMs (pointOnPath) so timing reads from the picture, not just the word. Expose window.state: scene (0-index), lives, score, sceneEnteredAt, qte, inputFlash,lastInputCode; expose window.game.reset. Loop: draw bg, cycle sprite frames ~7 FPS, draw huge prompt, decay inputFlash. Keydown computes openNow from performance.now()-sceneEnteredAt, sets lastInputCode/inputFlash immediately, then correct key advances, wrong/missed key costs life. HUD displays scene+1. Videos are up-front media: muted overlay, any key skips, onended/onerror continues, fallback to key art.

recipe.state: scene zero-index; lives; score; sceneEnteredAt; qte{active,windowOpen,expectKey,prompt,result,windowStartMs,durationMs}; inputFlash; lastInputCode
order: load generated bg/sprite/key-art/video paths into helpers
draw bg_scene with drawImage, then sprites
cycle generated frames ~7 FPS; move threat along hazardPath to the threatened part by hitMs (pointOnPath) so it visibly closes in
keydown computes openNow and sets inputFlash immediately
correct key advances; wrong/missed key decrements lives
R reset: scene=0, lives=3, score=0, no new listeners
traps: state.scene is 0-indexed; never write probes requiring scene>=1 at page load
blocking title screen makes harness see no input; start playable or make title key visibly change state
do not add key listeners inside reset/enterScene/startGame
pink MISSING markers or giant fillRect backgrounds are not final art
after patch rejection, replace one full function with balanced braces
threat sits static until the keypress: move it along hazardPath toward the threatened part across cue->hit so timing reads from the picture
tuning: frame ~140 ms (7 FPS); cue/windup 300-700 ms before the window
first scene window opens immediately or keydown computes it
qte window 800-1800 ms shrinking; 3 lives; threat reaches threatened part at hitMs
probes: state exists; scene is numeric; R resets scene=0/lives=3/score=0
expected key IN window changes scene/qte.result/score/lives/inputFlash; same key OUT of window scores no success
at least one generated bg/sprite is drawn with drawImage; threat draw position changes between cue and hit (visible approach)

content: For a card/tabletop game (solitaire/freecell/memory): state.piles = arrays of card objects {rank, suit, faceUp}; render each pile as overlapping cards at a fixed y-offset, drawing each face with its rank+suit (text/symbol) on a rounded-rect, NOT a blank box. Mouse: pointerdown hit-tests the topmost card under the cursor, lifts it (and any valid stack on top) following the pointer; pointerup drops onto a legal target pile (validate the move rule) or snaps back. Deck/stock: click deals to waste/hand. Expose state.piles (or state.deck/state.tableau) on window. Win when foundations complete. R reshuffles + redeals. Probes: a pointerdown on a card changes a 'dragging' field; a legal drop changes which pile holds the card.

recipe.state: piles{name:[{rank,suit,faceUp}]}; dragging{cards[],fromPile,offsetX,offsetY}|null; won
order: validate lift rule, store dragging + pointer offset
pointerup: hit-test target pile, validate drop rule
win check, draw piles fanned, dragging last
traps: cards drawn as blank boxes: render rank+suit text on a rounded rect
hit-test bottom-up grabs a buried card: scan from the visually topmost down
drop committed without validating the move rule: validate, else snap back
dragged cards drawn under piles: draw the dragging stack last
ignoring the pointer-grab offset: cards jump so their corner sits at the cursor
tuning: fan offset 20-30 px faceUp, 8-12 px faceDown
probes: pointerdown on a card sets a dragging field
a legal drop changes which pile holds the card

content: For a physics/projectile game (slingshot/cannon/artillery): state.projectile = {x, y, vx, vy, launched}. Aim phase: drag (or angle+power keys) sets the launch vector; show a faint predicted arc. On launch, integrate each frame: vy += GRAVITY*dt; x += vx*dt; y += vy*dt. Resolve collisions with targets/structures (AABB or circle); on hit, apply damage/topple and award score. Targets: state.targets[] with hp/standing. Ground + walls clamp. Expose state.projectile and state.targets on window. Win when all targets cleared; lose when out of shots. R resets projectile, targets, score. Probes: after launch the projectile's y changes non-linearly (gravity); a hit decrements a target or score changes.

recipe.state: projectile{x,y,vx,vy,launched}; targets[]{x,y,hp,standing}; shots; aiming{angle,power}
order: aim: drag or keys set angle/power
draw predicted arc with the SAME integrator
each frame: vy += G*dt, integrate x,y
off-screen or rest -> next shot; win/lose; R resets all
traps: launch velocity re-applied every frame: set once at launch, then only integrate
arc preview computed with different math than flight: reuse one step function
culling off-screen before collision check: collide first, then cull
aim-phase zero gravity leaking into flight: gate physics on launched flag
R resets the projectile but not targets/shots/score: reset the whole round
probes: after launch, y changes non-linearly (gravity)
a hit decrements target hp or changes score

content: For a tower-defense game: enemies follow a fixed waypoint path across the map; towers placed on free tiles auto-fire at enemies in range; leaked enemies cost lives; kills pay money for more/better towers; waves escalate.

recipe.state: path[]{x,y} waypoints; enemies[]{pathIdx,t,hp,speed}; towers[]{col,row,range,cooldown}; wave; money; lives
order: towers tick cooldown, pick a live target in range, fire
apply damage; dead enemies pay money
enemy reaching the last waypoint costs a life
draw path, towers, ranges, enemies, HUD
traps: enemies steered by pixels not waypoints: track pathIdx + interpolation t, never freestyle
tower fires every frame: enforce a cooldown timer per tower
towers placeable ON the path: forbid path tiles at placement validation
firing at already-dead enemies: re-validate the target each shot
whole wave spawned in one frame: stagger spawns with an interval timer
probes: enemies[0].pathIdx increases over time
placing a tower deducts money
an enemy in range loses hp

content: For a match-3 puzzle: swap two adjacent gems; if the swap makes a 3+ run it clears, gems above drop down, new gems refill from the top, and cascades chain until the board is stable; otherwise the swap reverts.

recipe.state: board[r][c] gem ids; selected{r,c}|null; animating; score; movesLeft (or timer)
order: click/drag selects then swaps adjacent gems
tentatively swap, scan rows AND cols for 3+ runs
gravity drops gems, refill from the top
traps: accepting swaps that produce no match: revert the swap
scanning only rows: check rows AND columns for runs
refilling before drops finish: resolve clear->drop->refill fully each pass
cascades not re-scored: loop until stable, scoring every pass (with multiplier)
input during animation corrupts the board: gate on an animating flag
tuning: swap anim 120-200 ms, cascade step 150-250 ms
probes: a legal swap changes the board
score increases on a match
board has no empty cells after resolution

content: For a rhythm/timing game: notes scroll toward a hit line in lanes; pressing the lane key inside a timing window judges perfect/good/miss by how close the press is to the note time; combos build score; missed notes break the combo.

recipe.state: notes[]{lane,timeMs,hit}; songTime; combo; score; judgments{perfect,good,miss}
order: songTime advances from elapsed ms (or AudioContext.currentTime)
keydown: find nearest unhit note in that lane
notes past the window become misses, combo resets
draw lanes, notes, hit line, judgment text, combo
traps: timing from frame counts: RAF jitters - always use elapsed milliseconds
the same note hittable twice: mark hit=true immediately
two close notes judged wrongly: pick the one with minimum |delta|
missed notes never cleaned up: cull passed notes and count the miss
audio start latency desyncs the chart: add a calibration offset constant
tuning: notes visible 1.5-2.5 s before the hit line
probes: note y positions change with time
a key press in the window raises score/combo
a missed note resets combo to 0

content: For a stealth game: guards patrol fixed waypoints with a vision cone; the player is detected only when inside the cone angle and range AND the line of sight is not blocked by walls; alert fills up, full alert triggers chase; reach the goal unseen.

recipe.state: player{x,y}; guards[]{x,y,dir,patrol[],state,alertTimer}; walls[]{x,y,w,h}; goal{x,y}
order: guards advance toward their next patrol waypoint
vision test: within cone angle AND range AND no wall segment blocks the line
seen: alert meter fills; full -> state='chase'
lost sight: cooldown back to patrol
draw map, vision cones, actors, alert UI
traps: vision ignoring walls: do a segment-vs-rect intersection test for line of sight
instant detection feels unfair: use an alert meter that fills over ~0.5-1 s
drawn cone not matching detection math: derive both from the same angle+range
guards vibrate at waypoints: use an arrive radius before switching targets
chasing through walls: block guard movement with the same wall collision as the player
probes: a guard's state flips to alert when player stands in the cone
a wall between them prevents detection
reaching the goal sets win state

content: For a roguelike dungeon crawler: procedurally generate non-overlapping rooms connected by L-shaped corridors; strictly turn-based movement (monsters step only when the player steps); fog of war reveals tiles around the player; stairs descend to a regenerated, harder floor.

recipe.state: map[r][c] (wall|floor); rooms[]; player{tx,ty,hp,inv[]}; monsters[]{tx,ty,hp}; seen[r][c]; depth
order: connect each room to the previous with an L corridor
spawn player, stairs, monsters, loot ON floor tiles
turn loop: player keydown moves/attacks one tile
then every monster takes one step (toward player if visible)
update seen[][] in a radius around the player
stairs descend -> regenerate harder floor; draw seen dim, visible bright
traps: unconnected rooms strand the player: connect sequentially so all are reachable
spawning entities inside walls: pick spawn cells from floor tiles only
monsters moving in real time: strictly one monster step per player step
fog never updated: recompute visible tiles after every player move
bump-attack and move both firing on one keypress: a move into a monster IS the attack
tuning: 5-9 rooms of 4-8 tiles on a 40x30 map
probes: floor tiles form one connected region
arrow key moves player 1 tile and monsters step once
seen[][] true-count grows as the player explores

content: For a pinball table: gravity pulls the ball down a sloped table; two flippers at the bottom rotate up on key press to bat the ball; bumpers reflect with an impulse and score; draining between the flippers loses the ball; a plunger relaunches.

recipe.state: ball{x,y,vx,vy}; flippers[2]{angle,active}; bumpers[]{x,y,r}; score; ballsLeft; launched
order: flipper keys: animate angle up fast, drop on release
integrate position in 2-4 substeps when fast
collide: walls reflect, bumpers reflect + impulse + score
flipper contact reflects, extra speed while flipper rising
below flippers = drain: ballsLeft--, relaunch via plunger
draw table, flippers, bumpers, ball, score
traps: ball tunnels through flippers at speed: substep the integration or cap speed
reflecting while still overlapping re-triggers: push the ball out of the shape first
flipper treated as a static wall: add upward boost when it is moving
energy gained every bounce: damp reflections ~0.99 except bumpers
drain gap wider than the flippers reach: align flipper tips to guard it
probes: ball falls under gravity when idle
flipper key changes the flipper angle
a bumper hit increases score

content: For an idle/clicker game: clicking earns currency; generators bought with currency produce passively per second; costs grow exponentially per purchase; upgrades multiply rates; prestige resets progress for a permanent multiplier.

recipe.state: currency; perClick; generators[]{count,baseCost,rate}; multiplier; lastTick
order: every frame: currency += totalRate * dt (dt capped)
buy: cost = baseCost * growth^count; validate then deduct + increment
upgrades raise perClick or generator rates
prestige available past threshold: reset, keep permanent multiplier
draw counters and buttons with affordability states
traps: costs not scaling: multiply by 1.07-1.15 per purchase or one generator dominates
per-frame income without dt: production becomes frame-rate dependent
huge numbers unformatted: display 1.2K/3.4M/5.6B suffixes past 4 digits
buttons clickable when unaffordable: disable visually AND validate in the handler
tab-blur dt spike pays a fortune: cap dt (idle gain is a design choice, not a bug)
tuning: first generator pays for itself in 10-30 s
probes: clicking increases currency
buying increments generator count and deducts cost
currency grows hands-off once a generator is owned

content: For a bullet-hell game: a boss emits scripted bullet patterns (spirals, rings, aimed shots) on timers; the player dodges with a tiny core hitbox much smaller than the sprite; boss phases switch patterns at hp thresholds.

recipe.state: player{x,y,r:2-4px core}; bullets[]{x,y,angle,speed}; boss{x,y,hp,phase,patternTimer}; lives; score
order: player moves (optional slow/focus key halves speed)
boss pattern script emits on timers: spiral angle+=step; ring = N evenly spaced; aimed = atan2 to player
collide bullets vs the SMALL core hitbox
player shots damage boss; phase switches at hp thresholds
draw bullets last so they stay visible
traps: hitbox = sprite size: use a 2-4 px core or it feels unfair
bullet array grows unbounded: cull off-screen aggressively and cap count
degrees mixed with radians in pattern math: pick radians everywhere
bullets spawning on top of the player: enforce a grace distance from spawn
phase switch mid-screen leaves an undodgeable wall: optionally clear bullets on switch
probes: bullets.length grows while a pattern runs
player survives sprite-graze, dies on core overlap
boss hp drops and phase changes at thresholds

content: For a word/typing game: letters typed on the keyboard build or match words under time pressure - falling words must be typed before landing, or guesses are committed with Enter and judged per letter (correct place / present / absent).

recipe.state: target (word or list); typed; guesses[]; fallingWords[]{word,x,y,progress}; score/wpm; timer; over
order: keydown: accept only e.key of length 1 (letters), normalize case
append to typed / advance the locked falling word
Backspace edits; Enter commits a guess
validate vs target or dictionary list
timer pressure: words fall or clock ticks; end on win/lives/time
traps: using e.code for letters: e.code is layout ('KeyA'); use e.key and filter length===1
case mismatch fails matches: normalize everything to lower case
wordle repeated letters scored naively: two-pass with a letter-count pool
typing splits across several falling words: lock to one word at first letter
browser shortcuts fire (space scrolls, / searches): preventDefault on game keys
tuning: fall speed 30-80 px/s, faster per level
probes: typing letters updates state.typed
Enter commits a guess and colors feedback
score/wpm changes when a word completes

content: For a stacking/balance game: a crane or hand swings above the stack; dropping releases the block with the crane's velocity; blocks settle if sufficiently supported by the one below, topple or slide off when overhanging too far; height is score.

recipe.state: blocks[]{x,y,w,h,vx,vy,resting}; crane{x,t,holding}; stackHeight; score; lives; over
order: drop key releases the block with the crane's velocity
land when overlapping the top block (or ground)
compute overlap: support >= threshold rests; less topples/slides off
camera/view rises with the stack; swing speeds up per block
miss or topple costs a life; draw
traps: requiring pixel-perfect landings: snap into place when offset is small - forgive
support computed against the whole stack: compare with the block directly below
resting blocks still integrating: freeze settled blocks or the stack jitters apart
camera never rises: tall stacks leave the playable view
constant swing speed: increase speed or amplitude per block for difficulty
probes: drop adds a block that lands on the stack
a barely-overlapping block falls or topples
stack height/score increases after a good drop
